query: we just landed our Oracle Fusion ERP data in Databricks — how is it structured and what should I watch out for?
expected_behavior: Loads oracle-fusion-overview as the foundation skill
Explains the Ledger / Legal-Entity / Business-Unit org model and the subledger->GL bridge
States the landing-agnostic rule: BICC PVO / FDI physical names vary; the canonical model is fixed and the glossary maps them

Surfaces the universal gotchas (multi-org _ALL scoping, CCID segments are customer config, accounting vs transaction date, period open/close, entered vs accounted currency, GL<->XLA double-count, posted-vs-unposted)
Offers to run oracle-fusion-setup to build the glossary and segment map
anti_behavior: Does NOT promise direct JDBC/ODBC access to live Fusion tables (it is SaaS)
Does NOT hard-code a physical table name without referencing the glossary
